---
title: "Consumer Complaint Analytics — Bank of America"
description: "A Voting Classifier achieving 86.1% precision on 61,022 CFPB complaints, deployed as a live Streamlit dashboard with projected net savings of $470,500 and an ROI of 1,191%."
author: "Ruairí Cullen"
date: "2024-12-01"
image: "../../images/complaints.png"
categories: [Machine Learning, NLP, Python, Streamlit, Prescriptive Analytics]
---

## Overview

This project transforms Bank of America's complaint management from a slow reactive 
First-In-First-Out system into an AI-powered fast-track routing system. Using 61,022 
real complaints from the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB), a Voting Classifier 
combining Histogram-based Gradient Boosting and Random Forest achieves **86.1% precision** 
identifying complaints likely to require monetary relief.

::: {.callout-tip}
🚀 **Live Dashboard:** [analysisofcomplaints.streamlit.app](https://analysisofcomplaints.streamlit.app)
:::

---

## Key Results

| Metric | Result |
|---|---|
| Model | Voting Classifier (Gradient Boosting + Random Forest) |
| Precision | 86.1% |
| ROC-AUC | 0.794 |
| Accuracy | 77.4% |
| Dataset Size | 61,022 CFPB complaints |
| Projected Gross Savings | $510,000 |
| Operational Cost | $39,500 |
| **Net Benefit** | **$470,500** |
| **ROI** | **1,191%** |

---

## Feature Engineering

The pipeline engineered the following features from raw CFPB data:

- **TF-IDF vectorisation** of complaint Issue text (unigrams + bigrams, 1,000 features)
- **Temporal features** — Days_to_Forward, Month_Sin, Month_Cos, Day_of_Week, Quarter
- **Risk rate features** — Product_Risk_Rate, State_Risk_Rate (historical payout probabilities)
- **Text metadata** — Issue_Length, Issue_Word_Count

---

## Python Code — Imports & Configuration

```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show imports"
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

# Unified colour theme
THEME = {
    'primary': '#4C9AFF',
    'secondary': '#A78BFA',
    'success': '#34D399',
    'danger': '#F87171',
    'warning': '#FBBF24',
    'neutral': '#475569',
}
print("Libraries loaded.")
```

## Python Code — Model Performance Visualisation

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Voting Classifier performance metrics on the CFPB test set"
#| echo: true

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

metrics = ['Precision', 'ROC-AUC', 'Accuracy', 'Recall']
values  = [86.1, 79.4, 77.4, 4.6]
colors  = ['#4C9AFF', '#A78BFA', '#34D399', '#F87171']

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 4))
bars = ax.barh(metrics, values, color=colors, edgecolor='white', height=0.5)
ax.set_xlim(0, 105)
ax.set_xlabel('Score (%)', fontsize=11)
ax.set_title('Voting Classifier — Model Performance Metrics',
             fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', pad=15)
ax.axvline(x=80, color='grey', linestyle='--', alpha=0.5, label='80% benchmark')
for bar, val in zip(bars, values):
    ax.text(val + 1, bar.get_y() + bar.get_height() / 2,
            f'{val}%', va='center', fontweight='bold', fontsize=10)
ax.legend(fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Python Code — Financial Simulation

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Net benefit and ROI across different risk thresholds"
#| echo: true

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Simulation parameters (from report)
COST_PER_REVIEW   = 50
AVG_PAYOUT_SAVED  = 750
flagged_cases     = 1020

thresholds    = np.arange(0.3, 0.8, 0.05)
gross_savings = [flagged_cases * AVG_PAYOUT_SAVED * (t * 0.9) for t in thresholds]
op_costs      = [flagged_cases * COST_PER_REVIEW * (1 - t * 0.3) for t in thresholds]
net_benefit   = [g - o for g, o in zip(gross_savings, op_costs)]
roi           = [(n / o) * 100 for n, o in zip(net_benefit, op_costs)]

fig, ax1 = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 4))
ax2 = ax1.twinx()

ax1.plot(thresholds, net_benefit, 'b-o', linewidth=2, label='Net Benefit ($)')
ax1.set_xlabel('Risk Threshold', fontsize=11)
ax1.set_ylabel('Net Benefit ($)', color='blue', fontsize=11)
ax1.tick_params(axis='y', labelcolor='blue')

ax2.plot(thresholds, roi, 'g--s', linewidth=2, label='ROI (%)')
ax2.set_ylabel('ROI (%)', color='green', fontsize=11)
ax2.tick_params(axis='y', labelcolor='green')

ax1.axvline(x=0.5, color='red', linestyle=':', alpha=0.6, label='Selected threshold (0.50)')
ax1.set_title('Financial Impact by Risk Threshold', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', pad=15)
fig.legend(loc='upper right', bbox_to_anchor=(0.88, 0.88), fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## Dashboard Features

The deployed Streamlit application serves three user groups:

| User | View | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Frontline Agents | Live Risk Assessment | Real-time monetary liability score per complaint |
| Managers | Operational Efficiency | Processing times and workload heatmaps |
| Executives | Market Intelligence | Volume patterns, financial exposure, ROI tracking |

---

## Ethical Considerations

::: {.callout-warning appearance="minimal"}
All Personally Identifiable Information (PII) was removed prior to analysis. Sensitive 
demographic variables — race, gender, and exact location — were excluded to ensure 
fair, bias-free predictions based solely on complaint content and product metadata.
:::

---

## Skills Used

`Python` `scikit-learn` `Gradient Boosting` `Random Forest` `TF-IDF` `NLP`
`Streamlit` `Prescriptive Analytics` `Ethical AI` `CFPB Data`

---

## References

- Chen & Guestrin (2016). XGBoost: A Scalable Tree Boosting System. *ACM SIGKDD*.
- Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (2024). [Consumer Complaint Database](https://www.consumerfinance.gov/data-research/consumer-complaints/).
